commands.description: Returns the size of a rectangle whose edges are at the boundaries of the range, useful for positioning custom tooltips or UI overlays over selected text.
Wraps the contents of the range in a new DOM node. Note: This will throw a DOMException if the range partially intersects with other nodes.
Sets the boundary of the range relative to the node itself rather than an offset within the node, useful for selecting entire elements.
Returns the text content of the range as a string, providing a quick way to extract raw text from a selection without manual traversal.
Static method used to compare the relative positions of the boundaries of two ranges (e.g., checking if one range starts before another).
